records.ety: [L. indistinctus: cf. F. indistinct. See In- not, and Distinct.]

defs: Not distinct or distinguishable; not separate in such a manner as to be perceptible by itself; as, the indistinct parts of a substance.
Obscure to the mind or senses; not clear; not definite; confused; imperfect; faint; as, indistinct vision; an indistinct sound; an indistinct idea or recollection.
quotes: When we come to parts too small four our senses, our ideas of these little bodies become obscure and indistinct.
Their views, indeed, are indistinct and dim.
